Project title: Strengthening Support Program for Parliamentary Functions
Description: The overall objective of the program is to help improve the operation of the Chilean Parliament by developing the Library of Congress of Chile and to promote a closer relationship between Congress and the Chilean people. The specific objectives, in the context of the comprehensive transformation to which the BCN is committed, are: (i) to develop and implement a management strategy that brings the BCN's internal organization and capabilities into line with its institutional vision; (ii) to build the information technology (IT) architecture needed to provide IT services; (iii) to redefine the support and advisory services for the legislature; and (iv) to help solidify the relationship between Congress and the Chilean people.The program's structure consists of four components: (i) strengthening of the management capacity of the Library of Congress; (ii) modernization of the IT architecture and website; (iii) innovation in the advisory services to support the parliamentary function; and (iv) strengthening of the relationship between Congress and the Chilean people.The restructuring process seeks to develop skills at the BCN to coordinate efforts that will make it possible to address topics that may be of interest to members of Parliament, the legislative community, or the public (demand), whether this is because they are recurring on the legislative agenda (e.g., foreign trade, regulation of utilities), because they are issues on the national agenda (e.g., income distribution, human rights, ethics, environmental regulations), or because they are innovative issues relevant throughout the world about which it is deemed necessary to promote awareness and discussion in the country (e.g., biotechnology, cloning)-all from the perspective of the impact on national coexistence. During formulation a primary survey of the demand for the project was conducted through interviews with members of Parliament and research institutions of the legislative community, the gathering of relevant documents and opinions from members of the BCN, and as part of the Logical Framework Workshop.
Purpose name: Public sector policy and administrative management

With these three components, the project will support three complementary areas: the internal capacities of the DNSC, the effective implementation of the new legislation regarding the senior executive service and the proffesionalization of human resource management in the Central Government

Project title: SheTrades Latin America: Supporting women businesses‘ transition towards the post COVID digital age (B999)
Description: SheTrades Latin America is a pilot initiative of ITC's SheTrades Global and ecomConnect programme running from June 2021 to June 2023 with the objective of increasing the digitalisation of Latin American women-led-businesses from Argentina, Chile, Colombia and Uruguay to sell online. ITC will work with trade-related partner institutions in the four countries by equipping them with tools and methodologies in Spanish to guide and assess a total of 1,000 beneficiary companies in their e-commerce journey on the virtual learning platform developed by the project. Doing so, the initiative will contribute to SDG 5 and the ITC target of Connecting 3 Million of Women to Markets.SheTrades Latinoamérica es una iniciativa piloto de SheTrades Global y ecomConnect del ITC entre junio de 2021 y junio de 2023 cuyo objetivo es aumentar la digitalización de las empresas latinoamericanas lideradas por mujeres en Argentina, Chile, Colombia y Uruguay para vender en línea. El ITC trabajará con instituciones de promoción del comercio en los cuatro países y las equipará con herramientas y metodologías en español para guiar e evaluar a un total de 1000 empresas beneficiarias en su ruta de e-commerce en la plataforma virtual de aprendizaje desarrollada por el proyecto. De este modo, la iniciativa contribuirá al objetivo del ITC de Conectar a 3 millones de mujeres a los mercados.

Project title: National Mining Policy: Support for a National Participatory Process in Chile
Description: The project aims to continuously improve the socio-economic contribution and environmental performance of the mining sector in Chile, by supporting the development and implementation of the National Mining Policy (NMP). The project will support a participatory process of stakeholders from government, industry, academia, communities and civil society organizations in the regions of Chile in the preparation of the NMP and the implementation of priority actions identified in that process. It will do by (i) supporting implementation of a series of stakeholder engagement events throughout the country, facilitated by a team of experts, aimed at gathering input for the NMP and ensuring the the policy reflects a wide range of expectations and sensitivities, (ii) expert technical round tables on key themes, to identify concrete SMART indicators and responsible entities to take forward the NMP, (iii) formulation of a project profile aimed at making actionable at least one priority action identified in the engagement process, and (iv) supporting a broad communication and transparency effort to ensure the policy is known and supported by key constituencies.

Project title: Strengthening the recovery and response capacity of the post-COVID-19 health system.
Description: The general objective is to contribute to the recovery of the public health system so that it responds better to the population's demand for care and achieves a better user experience.The specific objectives are: (1) Contribute to improving the quality of health care through the development of new standards based on a people-centered health model, which can be tested in primary care centers and can be scaled to the rest of the system; (2) Contribute to the preventive approach of common chronic diseases and their risk factors by expanding the coverage of remote assistance to meet the demand for care of pathologies that require permanent monitoring, in particular high blood pressure, diabetes, chronic respiratory and kidney diseases .
ISIC description letter lvl: Human health and social work activities
Channel - name: Inter-American Development Bank, Inter-American Investment Corporation and Multilateral Investment Fund

Project title: Digital health to improve the quality of care for female users and female caregivers of chronic patients.
Description: The general objective of this TC is (1) to contribute to improving the opportunity in care through the development of digital health in Chile. Its specific objectives are (1) Support the resolution capacity in the health care network establishments in chronic diseases, cancer and other specialties with a high prevalence in women, by reinforcing the model of teleinterconsultations between the Digital Hospital and primary care centers; (2) Facilitate the work of women who are responsible for the primary care of relatives who receive palliative care, especially cancer patients, with advanced, progressive chronic disease, with a limited life expectancy and highly demanding family care; (3) Contribute to the formulation of quality standards for health care with a gender perspective.
ISIC description letter lvl: Human health and social work activities
Channel - name: Inter-American Development Bank, Inter-American Investment Corporation and Multilateral Investment Fund

Project title: Support for the Continuity of Essential Health Care with an Emphasis on the Management of Waiting Lists and Care for Chronic Patients in the Context of the COVID-19 Crisis
Description: 1) Contribute to the improvement of the capacity of provision of health care network with strategies that allow better case management and contribute to the continuity of essential care for priority populations during and after the emergency. 2) Contribute to better management of health waiting lists with the updating of the data model and the interoperability of the health waiting time management system, with other key repositories of the integrated network of health services. 3) Implement a telehealth model in primary health care centers that allows clinical teams to maintain regular control over their patients with chronic diseases to avoid decompensation.
ISIC description letter lvl: Human health and social work activities
Channel - name: Inter-American Development Bank, Inter-American Investment Corporation and Multilateral Investment Fund

Project title: Strengthening the National Civil Service Department
Description: The Program's objective is to deepen the modernization and professionalization of public human resource management in Chile, by strengthening the institutional capacity of the National Civil Service Department (DNSC).The program will finance activities in three components: (i) Institutional capacity building in the DNSC, (ii) Support to the implementation of the Senior Executive Service, and (iii) Support to the modernization of human resource management in the Chilean Civil Service at the Central Government. With these three components, the project will support three complementary areas: the internal capacities of the DNSC, the effective implementation of the new legislation regarding the senior executive service and the proffesionalization of human resource management in the Central Government
Purpose name: Public sector policy and administrative management
ISIC description letter lvl: Public administration and defence; compulsory social security

Project title: Strengthening Support Program for Parliamentary Functions
Description: The overall objective of the program is to help improve the operation of the Chilean Parliament by developing the Library of Congress of Chile and to promote a closer relationship between Congress and the Chilean people. The specific objectives, in the context of the comprehensive transformation to which the BCN is committed, are: (i) to develop and implement a management strategy that brings the BCN's internal organization and capabilities into line with its institutional vision; (ii) to build the information technology (IT) architecture needed to provide IT services; (iii) to redefine the support and advisory services for the legislature; and (iv) to help solidify the relationship between Congress and the Chilean people.The program's structure consists of four components: (i) strengthening of the management capacity of the Library of Congress; (ii) modernization of the IT architecture and website; (iii) innovation in the advisory services to support the parliamentary function; and (iv) strengthening of the relationship between Congress and the Chilean people.The restructuring process seeks to develop skills at the BCN to coordinate efforts that will make it possible to address topics that may be of interest to members of Parliament, the legislative community, or the public (demand), whether this is because they are recurring on the legislative agenda (e.g., foreign trade, regulation of utilities), because they are issues on the national agenda (e.g., income distribution, human rights, ethics, environmental regulations), or because they are innovative issues relevant throughout the world about which it is deemed necessary to promote awareness and discussion in the country (e.g., biotechnology, cloning)-all from the perspective of the impact on national coexistence. During formulation a primary survey of the demand for the project was conducted through interviews with members of Parliament and research institutions of the legislative community, the gathering of relevant documents and opinions from members of the BCN, and as part of the Logical Framework Workshop.
Purpose name: Public sector policy and administrative management

Project title: Support for an Effective Descentralization
Description: Support SUBDERE in the implementation of its regionalization strengthening program based on the reform that would implement Law No. 21,074 on the Constitutional Government and Regional Administration. Project title: Support for Chiles Public Integrity and Transparency Agenda
Description: The general objective of this TC is to contribute to the efforts of the Government of Chile to continue strengthening and consolidating the legal and institutional framework for the prevention and control of corruption and the promotion of transparency. Specifically, this TC will enable studies, generate policy management instruments, and develop awareness-raising and training activities that enable the implementation of a set of medium to long term policy actions and gradual implementation, which the Government of Chile has been defining in the last two years; some of which directly contribute to mitigate the risks associated with the declared health emergency in the context of the Covid-19 pandemic. In addition to transparency and accountability, in crisis contexts more and more information, updated and from different sources, is also required to strengthen decision-making.

Provider agency: Multi-Donor Fund for the Transformation of Technical and Vocational Education and Training
Project title: Smart Job Retraining
Description: The objective of this TC is to contribute to the development of a technological platform that based on machine learning allows linking the interests and demands of people and organizations. In other words, it aims at designing a technological platform called 'Intelligent Labor Retraining' or RELINT (in Spanish), based on a data intelligence model that allows companies and individuals to be guided in the process of retraining. On the one hand, supporting companies in a digital transformation process by introducing reskilling, upskilling and outplacement mechanisms and, on the other hand, allowing workers to improve their skills for a new type of company or to start a new labor route . This is done by linking interests, experience and employability potential with the demand that the market is requesting for new skills derived from the ongoing digital transformation.

Project title: Support to the Development of New Transport Technology Applications in Chile: Big Data and Autonomous Vehicles
Description: Main objective: Improve the instruments of regulation, management and planning of transport systems, through the use of digital data collection and autonomous vehicles technologies. a) Design and implementation of a technology-based pilot for the collection of mobility patterns for transport planning. B) Design strategies to promote the participation of people in data collection platforms for planning transport. c) Study the enabling conditions in regulation to facilitate the development of autonomous transport for passengers and cargo. d) Encourage instances for the implementation of assisted or autonomous driving vehicles pilots. e) Develop recommendations and propose strategies for the adoption of emerging technologies applied to transport and mobility. f) Study governance, roles and technical capacities that allows Chilean Government to face the challenges imposed by the technological advances.

Project title: Movistar Chile Handset Financing
Description: The Project consists of an uncommitted receivables purchase facility for US$100 million (Facility) to sell mobile phone handset receivables originated by Telefonica Móviles Chile S.A.  (the Company or Movistar Chile). The Facility will have an availability period of 48 months. The receivables have a repayment period of up to 24 months from the issuance date of the lease contract. Under the Facility, IDB Invest shall purchase, in its sole discretion, the mobile phone handset receivables.The Project seeks to support the adoption and use of smartphones that allow the final subscribers of Movistar Chile to access to data services. Greater access to data services has a positive impact on economic development since broadband penetration facilitates the creation of jobs, promotes the creation of businesses, increases productivity, and positively impacts per capita growth. However, there are important barriers to deal with dramatic growth in data uses, one of which is the cost of acquiring equipment and technologies that allow end users to use Internet services. The proposed Project is one of the ways that IDB Invest will seek to address this barrier.
ISIC description letter lvl: Information and communication

The Line of Credit will be available for a period of up to 48 months and the invoices have a cancellation period of up to 24 months.

Project title: Tanner SMEs Financing Partnership
Description: The Project aims to finance small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in Chile. The proposed operation consists of a credit facility for approximately US$140 million. The loan will have a duration of up to five years, consisting of: (i) an A Loan of up to US$20 million that will be funded by IDB Invest; and (ii) a B Loan for approximately US$120 million to be funded by participating investors. The project will allow Tanner to improve the offer of its financial services to its clients, diversify its sources of financing and, at the same time, improve the structure of its assets and liabilities.
ISIC description letter lvl: Financial and insurance activities

Project title: AVLA - Social Housing Financing
Description: The proposed project includes a financing mechanism that will consist of a senior, secured, revolving warehouse facility of up to US$50 million, whose proceeds will be used to purchase and accumulate eligible endorsable mortgage loans or other similar instruments granted to individuals, with a particular focus on diversity and inclusion, for future total or partial asset-backed securitizations in the Chilean capital markets. The transaction will support and improve certain diversity and inclusion segments access to financing in Chile, narrowing the current financing gap that is increasing due to the COVID-19 crisis, and will help further expand debt capital markets in the country through new issuances of asset-backed securities. The proceeds from the line will allow AVLA Group to expand its origination of mortgage loans. The warehousing facility may support several accumulation periods, for a maximum total availability period of six years.
ISIC description letter lvl: Financial and insurance activities

Project title: Fondo Esperanza Microfinance Financing Partnership

Description: SheTrades Latin America is a pilot initiative of ITC's SheTrades Global and ecomConnect programme running from June 2021 to June 2023 with the objective of increasing the digitalisation of women-led-businesses in Latin America to enable them to sell online. ITC will work with technical partner institutions specialized in digital commerce in Argentina, Chile, Colombia and Uruguay to develop and pilot with them tools and methodologies in Spanish. The project aims to guide and assess a total of 1,000 beneficiary companies in their e-commerce journey on the virtual learning platform developed by the project. Doing so, the initiative will contribute to SDG 5 and the ITC target of Connecting 3 Million of Women to Markets. SheTrades Latin America es una iniciativa piloto de SheTrades Global y ecomConnect del ITC que se está ejecutando entre junio 2021 y junio 2023. Su objetivo es aumentar la digitalización de las empresas latinoamericanas lideradas por mujeres para vender en línea. El ITC trabajará con las instituciones técnicas especializadas en comercio digital en Argentina, Chile, Colombia y Uruguay para desarrollar y probar las herramientas y metodologías en español. El proyecto aspira a capacitar a un total de 1.000 empresas beneficiarias en su ruta para vender en línea utilizando la plataforma de aprendizaje virtual desarrollada en el marco del proyecto. Project title: Empresas Ariztia
Description: The proposed financing consists of a Long-term Senior Loan to Empresas Ariztía, for up to US$20 million and a maximum term of 10 years, for the company's investment program for the years 2021 and 2022, aimed at increasing production, implementing new technologies and efficiencies, improvements in terms of animal welfare, as well as their maintenance investments. Additionally, a second loan, which consists of a committed revolving line to finance working capital for up to US$30 million, with an availability period of 65 months, disbursements within 390 days, and intended to finance working capital related to the line bird genetics. Empresas Ariztía is one of the main poultry producers in Chile, with national and export sales, and value-added products in chickens, turkeys and cured meats. The company is vertically integrated in the production chain, from the production of the feed, through the incubation, fattening and processing of poultry meat, to the distribution and sale of the final product. In addition to poultry products, it also sells eggs, mushrooms, cheeses, butter, vegetables and other meats.

Description: We propose to carry out a project which will produce a decisive step towards improving the accuracy of the Hubble constant as determined from the Cepheid-SN Ia method to 1%, by using 28 extremely rare eclipsing binary systems in the LMC which offer the potential to determine their distances to 1%. Project title: Skills for Employment in the Extractives Sector of the Pacific Alliance/Compétences pour l'emploi dans le secteur extractif de l'Alliance du Pacifique
Description: The project supports Pacific Alliance countries (Chile, Colombia, Mexico and Peru) in strengthening their technical and vocational education and training, and establishing strong, demand-driven and industry-responsive training systems in the extractive sector.The project supports clean development and inclusive growth by collaborating with civil society and the private sector, and promoting responsible natural resource management and commercial practices. Project activities include: (1) providing training for the qualification and employability of vulnerable women, youth and Indigenous peoples, on environmental management and rehabilitation of mining sites; (2) sharing best practices in Canadian and Pacific Alliance institutions on self-employment; and (3) organizing thematic forums to disseminate policies developed on themes common to all Pacific Alliance countries. / Le projet appuie les pays de l’Alliance du Pacifique (Chili, Colombie, Mexique et Pérou) à renforcer l’enseignement et la formation techniques et professionnels et à mettre en place des systèmes de formation solides, axés sur la demande et adaptés aux besoins de l’industrie dans le secteur extractif.Le projet soutient le développement propre et la croissance inclusive en collaborant avec la société civile et le secteur privé et en encourageant une gestion responsable des ressources naturelles et des pratiques commerciales responsables. Parmi les activités du projet : 1) offrir une formation sur la qualification et l’employabilité des femmes, des jeunes et des peuples autochtones vulnérables sur la gestion de l’environnement et la réhabilitation des sites miniers; 2) partager les pratiques exemplaires des institutions du Canada et de l’Alliance du Pacifique sur le travail autonome; 3) organiser des forums thématiques pour diffuser les politiques élaborées sur des thèmes communs à tous les pays de l’Alliance du Pacifique.
ISIC description letter lvl: Education
Channel: Colleges and Institutes Canada(previously named ACCC)

Project title: Digital health to improve the quality of care for female users and female caregivers of chronic patients.
Description: The general objective of this TC is (1) to contribute to improving the opportunity in care through the development of digital health in Chile. Its specific objectives are (1) Support the resolution capacity in the health care network establishments in chronic diseases, cancer and other specialties with a high prevalence in women, by reinforcing the model of teleinterconsultations between the Digital Hospital and primary care centers; (2) Facilitate the work of women who are responsible for the primary care of relatives who receive palliative care, especially cancer patients, with advanced, progressive chronic disease, with a limited life expectancy and highly demanding family care; (3) Contribute to the formulation of quality standards for health care with a gender perspective.
ISIC description letter lvl: Human health and social work activities
Channel - name: Inter-American Development Bank, Inter-American Investment Corporation and Multilateral Investment Fund

Project title: Support for the strategy to strengthen mental health policy in Chile.
Description: The general objective of this technical cooperation is to contribute to the expansion of coverage and opportunity for mental health care in the country, through the construction of a regulatory framework that incorporates new elements into the service model and provides financing guarantees to enhance capacity response of the health system. The specific objective of the project is to generate knowledge products that contribute to the discussion for the formulation of the comprehensive mental health bill and other necessary initiatives to improve the response of the health system in this area.

Channel - name: Inter-American Development Bank, Inter-American Investment Corporation and Multilateral Investment Fund

Project title: TURQUESA-Countering migrant smuggling and human trafficking in the Americas/TURQUESA-Lutter contre le trafic de migrants et la traite des êtres humains dans les Amériques
Description: Project TURQUESA aims to counter migrant smuggling and human trafficking through effective investigations and prosecutions in the Americas in a joint initiative, implemented by the International Criminal Police Organization (INTERPOL) and the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC).The overarching objective of the project is to dismantle, disrupt and bring to justice organized crime groups and networks engaged in the smuggling of migrants (SOM) and Trafficking in Persons (TIP). It will do so by building the capacity of countries in the Americas to track, identify, investigate and prosecute organized criminal groups engaging in these and other related crimes. The project ensures sustainable benefits in the long term by strengthening the capacity of the law enforcement and criminal justice systems in individual countries to address migrant smuggling and human trafficking, improving their ability to engage and coordinate actions within and across national borders. / Le projet TURQUESA vise à lutter contre le trafic de migrants et la traite des êtres humains par le biais d'enquêtes et de poursuites efficaces dans les Amériques dans le cadre d'une initiative conjointe, mise en œuvre par l'Organisation internationale de police criminelle (INTERPOL) et l'Office des Nations Unies contre la drogue et le crime (ONUDC).L'objectif principal du projet est de démanteler, perturber et traduire en justice les groupes et réseaux criminels organisés impliqués dans le trafic de migrants et la traite des personnes. Pour ce faire, il renforcera la capacité des pays des Amériques à suivre, identifier, enquêter et poursuivre les groupes criminels organisés qui se livrent à ces crimes et à d'autres crimes connexes. Le projet garantit des avantages durables à long terme en renforçant la capacité des systèmes d'application de la loi et de justice pénale de chaque pays à lutter contre le trafic de migrants et la traite des êtres humains, en améliorant leur capacité à engager et à coordonner des actions à l'intérieur et au-delà des frontières nationales.
Purpose name: Facilitation of orderly, safe, regular and responsible migration and mobility
ISIC description letter lvl: Public administration and defence; compulsory social security

Project title: Strengthening the National Civil Service Department
Description: The Program's objective is to deepen the modernization and professionalization of public human resource management in Chile, by strengthening the institutional capacity of the National Civil Service Department (DNSC).The program will finance activities in three components: (i) Institutional capacity building in the DNSC, (ii) Support to the implementation of the Senior Executive Service, and (iii) Support to the modernization of human resource management in the Chilean Civil Service at the Central Government. With these three components, the project will support three complementary areas: the internal capacities of the DNSC, the effective implementation of the new legislation regarding the senior executive service and the proffesionalization of human resource management in the Central Government
Purpose name: Public sector policy and administrative management
ISIC description letter lvl: Public administration and defence; compulsory social security

Project title: Strengthening Support Program for Parliamentary Functions
Description: The overall objective of the program is to help improve the operation of the Chilean Parliament by developing the Library of Congress of Chile and to promote a closer relationship between Congress and the Chilean people. The specific objectives, in the context of the comprehensive transformation to which the BCN is committed, are: (i) to develop and implement a management strategy that brings the BCN's internal organization and capabilities into line with its institutional vision; (ii) to build the information technology (IT) architecture needed to provide IT services; (iii) to redefine the support and advisory services for the legislature; and (iv) to help solidify the relationship between Congress and the Chilean people.The program's structure consists of four components: (i) strengthening of the management capacity of the Library of Congress; (ii) modernization of the IT architecture and website; (iii) innovation in the advisory services to support the parliamentary function; and (iv) strengthening of the relationship between Congress and the Chilean people.The restructuring process seeks to develop skills at the BCN to coordinate efforts that will make it possible to address topics that may be of interest to members of Parliament, the legislative community, or the public (demand), whether this is because they are recurring on the legislative agenda (e.g., foreign trade, regulation of utilities), because they are issues on the national agenda (e.g., income distribution, human rights, ethics, environmental regulations), or because they are innovative issues relevant throughout the world about which it is deemed necessary to promote awareness and discussion in the country (e.g., biotechnology, cloning)-all from the perspective of the impact on national coexistence. During formulation a primary survey of the demand for the project was conducted through interviews with members of Parliament and research institutions of the legislative community, the gathering of relevant documents and opinions from members of the BCN, and as part of the Logical Framework Workshop.
Purpose name: Public sector policy and administrative management
ISIC description letter lvl: Public administration and defence; compulsory social security

Project title: Program to Strengthen the Strategic Management of Public Security in Chile
Description: The program's general objective is to help reduce violent robberies and improve Chile's capacity tomanage cyber incidents and cybercrimes. Its specific objectives are to: (i) improve police capacity to prevent and investigate violentrobberies in the Santiago metropolitan region; (ii) reduce antisocial and criminal behavior among young people (ages 10 through 17) inprioritized municipalities of the Santiago metropolitan region; and (iii) improve the capacity of the Ministry of the Interior and Public Securityto manage cyber incidents.
ISIC description letter lvl: Public administration and defence; compulsory social security
Channel: MINISTERIO DEL INTERIOR Y SEGURIDAD PUBLICA

Project title: Assisting Public Expenditure Quality and Efficiency
Description: The general objective of the TC is to assist the recovery from the health and fiscal crisis brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic, through strengthening the efficiency of public expenditure and the financial management of the public sector. The specific objectives of the TC are: i) to support the strengthening of the budget formulation system, and ii) assist the implementation of the Single Treasury Account.

Project title: Support the Chilean Police Reform

Project title: Inter-American Program for Strengthening Gender Equality in Counterdrug Law Enforcement Agencies/Renforcement de l'égalité des sexes dans les organismes chargés de l'application des lois antidrogue
Description: This project aims to reduce drug-related security threats for persons in selected beneficiary countries and Canadians. Project activities include: (1) Participation in six counterdrug regional/international events to present the Gender Equality in Counterdrug Law Enforcement Agencies (GENLEA) Program and Handbook; (2) One regional training on operational counterdrug intelligence for female law enforcement officers through the Regional Counterdrug Intelligence School of the Americas; and (3) Two regional trainings on the identification and control of precursor chemicals, New Psychoactive Substances (NPS) and synthetic opioids, including fentanyl and its analogues for drug and chemical control officers. The project will benefit the law enforcement community in Antigua and Barbuda, Argentina, The Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominica, Dominican Republic, Grenada, Guatemala, Guyana, Haiti, Jamaica, Mexico, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and The Grenadines, Suriname, Trinidad and Tobago, and Uruguay. / Ce projet vise à réduire les menaces de sécurité liées à la drogue pour les Canadiens et les personnes dans certains pays. Les activités de ce projet comprennent : 1) la participation à six événements régionaux/internationaux de lutte contre la drogue afin de présenter le Gender Equality in Counterdrug Law Enforcement Agencies Program and Handbook (en anglais seulement) aux personnes chargées de l’application de la loi en matière de lutte contre la drogue; 2) l’organisation d’une formation régionale sur le renseignement opérationnel en matière de lutte contre la drogue pour les femmes chargées de l’application de la loi par l’intermédiaire de l’École régionale de renseignement sur la lutte contre la drogue des Amériques (Regional Counterdrug Intelligence School of the Americas); 3) l’organisation de deux activités de formation régionales sur l’identification et le contrôle des précurseurs chimiques, des nouvelles substances psychoactives et des opioïdes synthétiques, y compris le fentanyl et ses analogues, pour les agents chargés du contrôle des drogues et des produits chimiques. Le projet devrait bénéficier aux services de police des pays suivants : Antigua-et-Barbuda, Argentine, Bahamas, Barbade, Belize, Bolivie, Brésil, Chili, Colombie, Costa Rica, Dominique, République dominicaine, Grenade, Guatemala, Guyana, Haïti, Jamaïque, Mexique, Panama, Paraguay, Pérou, Saint-Kitts-et-Nevis, Sainte-Lucie, Saint-Vincent-et-les-Grenadines, Suriname, Trinité-et-Tobago et Uruguay.
ISIC description letter lvl: Public administration and defence; compulsory social security

Project title: Countering Disinformation within Civic Education in Colombia and Chile - CoDECo/Contrer la désinformation dans l'éducation civique en Colombie et Chile - CoDECo.
Description: The CoDECo project aims to transform political awareness and engagement among Colombian civil society through civic education and strengthened resilience to politically-motivated disinformation campaigns which threaten democratic processes, specifically elections. The project will develop quality gender-responsive civic education and media literacy programming for delivery through the formal education system (grades 6-11) in 5 key districts in Colombia. A second track of work will provide gender-sensitive civic education and media literacy tools, training and resources for education stakeholders and professionals in country. / Le projet CoDECo vise à transformer la conscience et l'engagement politique de la société civile colombienne, grâce à l'éducation civique et en offrant une meilleure sensibilisation aux campagnes de désinformation qui menacent les processus démocratiques, en particulier les élections.  Le projet mettra au point des programmes d'éducation civique et d'éducation aux médias, tenant compte des sexospécificités.  Celles-ce seront dispensés par le biais du système éducatif formel (de la 6e à la 11e année) dans 5 districts clés de Colombie. Une deuxième piste de travail fournira des outils, une formation et des ressources d'éducation civique et d'éducation aux medias, sensibles au genre, pour les educateurs(trices) et les professionnels de l'éducation dans le pays.
ISIC description letter lvl: Public administration and defence; compulsory social security

Project title: Securing Marine and Land Based Trade and Commercial Cargo in OAS Member States/Sécurisation du fret maritime et terrestre dans les États membres d'OAS
Description: Following the excellent results of Project P001485, this project is the Phase II and aims to reduce vulnerability to transnational threats posed by international crime and terrorism exploiting cargo and trade as means of entry into participating Organization of American States member states. The project activities include: (1) strengthening capacities of law enforcement and customs officers responsible for securing and inspecting commercial cargo in land based ports of entry through introductory and advanced training relevant to their roles and responsibilities; (2) updating decision-makers, customs and law enforcement officials on international standards, good practices, lessons learned, and recent trends on cargo security, interagency cooperation, public-private partnerships, and protection of marine and land based ports of entry; and (3) establishing Inter-American roster of customs and cargo security experts. This project benefits states in Latin America and the Caribbean including Argentina, Barbados, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Haiti, Honduras, Jamaica, Mexico, Peru, and Trinidad & Tobago. / Suite aux excellents résultats du projet P001485, ce projet constitue la phase II et vise à réduire la vulnérabilité aux menaces transnationales posées par la criminalité internationale et le terrorisme qui exploitent le fret et le commerce comme moyens d'entrée dans les États membres de l'Organisation des États américains (OEA) participants. Les activités du projet comprennent (1) le renforcement des capacités des agents des douanes et des forces de l'ordre chargés de sécuriser et d'inspecter les cargaisons commerciales dans les ports d'entrée terrestres grâce à une formation initiale et avancée adaptée à leurs rôles et responsabilités ; (2) la mise à jour des décideurs, des agents des douanes et des forces de l'ordre sur les normes internationales, les bonnes pratiques, les enseignements tirés et les tendances récentes en matière de sécurité des cargaisons, de coopération interinstitutionnelle, de partenariats public-privé et de protection des ports d'entrée maritimes et terrestres ; et (3) l'établissement d'un fichier interaméricain d'experts en douanes et en sécurité des cargaisons. Ce projet bénéficie aux États d'Amérique latine et des Caraïbes, notamment l'Argentine, la Barbade, le Chili, la Colombie, le Costa Rica, la République dominicaine, Haïti, le Honduras, la Jamaïque, le Mexique, le Pérou et Trinité-et-Tobago.
Purpose name: Justice, law and order policy, planning and administration
ISIC description letter lvl: Public administration and defence; compulsory social security

Project title: Support for the diagnosis and strengthening of the line of action on migration issues of the Housing Emergency Plan and Containment of the Urban Deficit.
Description: The objective of the technical cooperation is to support the Ministry of Housing and Urban Planning (MINVU) of Chile in the preparatory activities for the implementation of the Plan de Emergencia Habitacional (PEH), especially in its dimension linked to the migrant population, through the PBR Support for the Housing Emergency Plan and Containment of the Urban Deficit (CH -L1173). This loan will contribute to the reduction of the housing deficit in Chile through (i) facilitating the collection and analysis of data flows and the housing deficit of the migrant population in order to improve capacity in the formulation of integration policies urban and social, and (ii) ensure access to housing for this target population.

Description: This technical cooperation aims to support the Ministerio de Vivienda y Urbanismo (MINVU) of Chile in the preparatory activities of the loan Support for the Housing Emergency Plan and Containment of the Urban Deficit (CH-L1173), which seeks to contribute to the reduction of the housing deficit that Chile through the : (i) development of comprehensive strategies for the regeneration of neighborhoods and deteriorating property; (ii) promotion of the production of industrialized housing with low-carbon urban and social infrastructure and non-traditional construction systems through planning and implementation of institutional frameworks; and (iii) strengthening of design, planning and execution capacities, improvement of tools and innovation in the ministry's technological systems in housing and land management programs.

Project title: Smart Job Retraining
Description: The objective of this TC is to contribute to the development of a technological platform that based on machine learning allows linking the interests and demands of people and organizations. In other words, it aims at designing a technological platform called Intelligent Labor Retraining or RELINT (in Spanish), based on a data intelligence model that allows companies and individuals to be guided in the process of retraining. On the one hand, supporting companies in a digital transformation process by introducing reskilling, upskilling and outplacement mechanisms and, on the other hand, allowing workers to improve their skills for a new type of company or to start a new labor route . This is done by linking interests, experience and employability potential with the demand that the market is requesting for new skills derived from the ongoing digital transformation.

Project title: Inter-American Program for Strengthening Gender Equality in Counterdrug Law Enforcement Agencies/Renforcement de l'égalité des sexes dans les organismes chargés de l'application des lois antidrogue
Description: This project aims to reduce drug-related security threats for persons in selected beneficiary countries and Canadians. Project activities include: (1) Participation in six counterdrug regional/international events to present the Gender Equality in Counterdrug Law Enforcement Agencies (GENLEA) Program and Handbook; (2) One regional training on operational counterdrug intelligence for female law enforcement officers through the Regional Counterdrug Intelligence School of the Americas; and (3) Two regional trainings on the identification and control of precursor chemicals, New Psychoactive Substances (NPS) and synthetic opioids, including fentanyl and its analogues for drug and chemical control officers. The project will benefit the law enforcement community in Antigua and Barbuda, Argentina, The Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominica, Dominican Republic, Grenada, Guatemala, Guyana, Haiti, Jamaica, Mexico, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and The Grenadines, Suriname, Trinidad and Tobago, and Uruguay. / Ce projet vise à réduire les menaces de sécurité liées à la drogue pour les Canadiens et les personnes dans certains pays. Les activités de ce projet comprennent : 1) la participation à six événements régionaux/internationaux de lutte contre la drogue afin de présenter le Gender Equality in Counterdrug Law Enforcement Agencies Program and Handbook (en anglais seulement) aux personnes chargées de l’application de la loi en matière de lutte contre la drogue; 2) l’organisation d’une formation régionale sur le renseignement opérationnel en matière de lutte contre la drogue pour les femmes chargées de l’application de la loi par l’intermédiaire de l’École régionale de renseignement sur la lutte contre la drogue des Amériques (Regional Counterdrug Intelligence School of the Americas); 3) l’organisation de deux activités de formation régionales sur l’identification et le contrôle des précurseurs chimiques, des nouvelles substances psychoactives et des opioïdes synthétiques, y compris le fentanyl et ses analogues, pour les agents chargés du contrôle des drogues et des produits chimiques. Le projet devrait bénéficier aux services de police des pays suivants : Antigua-et-Barbuda, Argentine, Bahamas, Barbade, Belize, Bolivie, Brésil, Chili, Colombie, Costa Rica, Dominique, République dominicaine, Grenade, Guatemala, Guyana, Haïti, Jamaïque, Mexique, Panama, Paraguay, Pérou, Saint-Kitts-et-Nevis, Sainte-Lucie, Saint-Vincent-et-les-Grenadines, Suriname, Trinité-et-Tobago et Uruguay.
ISIC description letter lvl: Public administration and defence; compulsory social security

Project title: E-Buses Chile
Description: The project consists of the financing of the purchase, supply, and maintenance of 992 electric buses (or E-buses) by Suministradora de Buses K Cuatro SpA (the SPV or the Company) for use in the Red Metropolitana de Movilidad (the RED), Santiago’s Public transport system (the Project). The E-buses will operate under a 14-year bus supply contract awarded as part of the ongoing fleet renewal and expansion tender carried out by the Ministry of Transport and Telecommunications of Chile (the MTT). The total Project cost is estimated at US$396 million (excluding VAT), which will be financed through a long-term structure composed of: (a) an IDB Invest senior loan for an aggregate amount of up to US$120 million and (b) parallel senior loans of up to US$183 million, which are expected to be Unidades de Fomento (UF)-linked loans. The rest of the Project’s costs will be funded through equity and subordinated debt contributions from the Sponsors. The Company is owned by Inversiones Kaufmann S.A., a subsidiary of Grupo Kaufmann, and Enel X Chile, a subsidiary of Enel Chile S.A. Each currently own 50% of the Company.This Project represents a relevant milestone in Chile’s Public transport system, since the country will have the largest E-buses urban fleet in the world outside of China.
SDG goal level explanation: IDB Invest is  committed to the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, adopted by the UN, and will strive to report on the SDG focus at target level for its portfolio in the coming years

Project title: Movistar Chile Handset Financing
Description: The Project consists of an uncommitted receivables purchase facility for US$100 million (Facility) to sell mobile phone handset receivables originated by Telefonica Móviles Chile S.A.  (the Company or Movistar Chile). The Facility will have an availability period of 48 months. The receivables have a repayment period of up to 24 months from the issuance date of the lease contract. Under the Facility, IDB Invest shall purchase, in its sole discretion, the mobile phone handset receivables.The Project seeks to support the adoption and use of smartphones that allow the final subscribers of Movistar Chile to access to data services. Greater access to data services has a positive impact on economic development since broadband penetration facilitates the creation of jobs, promotes the creation of businesses, increases productivity, and positively impacts per capita growth. However, there are important barriers to deal with dramatic growth in data uses, one of which is the cost of acquiring equipment and technologies that allow end users to use Internet services. The proposed Project is one of the ways that IDB Invest will seek to address this barrier.

Project title: End-Users Stabilization Tariffs 2022
Description: On August 2, 2022, Law No. 21,472 (the MPC Act), which creates the Tariff Stabilization Fund (the Fund) and establishes a New Transitional Electricity Price Stabilization Mechanism for Customers subject to Price Regulation, was published in the Official Gazette of the Republic of Chile. The MPC Act regulates the terms and conditions under which electricity tariffs are to be stabilized and paid in the future. The Fund is intended to pay the differences that may arise between invoicing by distribution companies (DisCos) to end customers for the energy and power component, and the amount payable to generation companies (GenCos) for the supply of electricity under the contractual conditions in force.

Project title: Latin America and  Caribbean Gender, Diversity, and Inclusion Fund – Technical Assistance/Fonds genre, diversité et inclusion en Amérique latine et dans les Caraïbes – Appui technique
Description: The BlueOrchard Latin America and Caribbean Gender, Diversity, and Inclusion Fund aims to increase the financial resilience of women, indigenous populations and people of African descent, as well as to support their material well-being and the growth of their businesses.The technical assistance mechanism supports client financial institutions of the fund in developing new financial inclusion solutions for women and in developing strategies to communicate with and effectively promote women clients. It also supports the development of diversity and inclusion policies within the human resources of the fund’s financial institution clients. Technical assistance supports the development or improvement of monitoring and reporting tools on gender, diversity, and inclusion; and helps financial institutions develop training on gender and inclusion for their employees. / Le Fonds Genre, diversité et inclusion en Amérique latine et dans les Caraïbes de BlueOrchard vise à accroître la résilience financière des femmes, des populations autochtones et des personnes d’ascendance africaine et à favoriser leur bien-être matériel et la croissance de leurs entreprises.Le mécanisme d`appui technique accompagnant le fonds appuie les institutions financières clientes du fonds à développer des nouvelles solutions d`inclusion financière pour inclure les femmes et à développer des stratégies pour communiquer avec les clientes et les promouvoir efficacement. Il soutient également le développement de politiques de diversité et d'inclusion dans les ressources humaines des institutions financières clientes du fonds. L'assistance technique soutient le développement ou l'amélioration des outils de suivi et de rapport sur le genre, la diversité et l'inclusion ; et aide les institutions financières à développer des formations sur les thèmes du genre et de l'inclusion pour leurs employés.
ISIC description letter lvl: Financial and insurance activities

Project title: AVLA - Social Housing Financing
Description: The proposed project includes a financing mechanism that will consist of a senior, secured, revolving warehouse facility of up to US$50 million, whose proceeds will be used to purchase and accumulate eligible endorsable mortgage loans or other similar instruments granted to individuals, with a particular focus on diversity and inclusion, for future total or partial asset-backed securitizations in the Chilean capital markets. The transaction will support and improve certain diversity and inclusion segments access to financing in Chile, narrowing the current financing gap that is increasing due to the COVID-19 crisis, and will help further expand debt capital markets in the country through new issuances of asset-backed securities. The proceeds from the line will allow AVLA Group to expand its origination of mortgage loans. The warehousing facility may support several accumulation periods, for a maximum total availability period of six years.

Project title: SheTrades Latin America: Supporting women businesses‘ transition towards the post COVID digital age (B999)
Description: SheTrades Latin America is a pilot initiative of ITC's SheTrades Global and ecomConnect programme running from June 2021 to June 2023 with the objective of increasing the digitalisation of women-led-businesses in Latin America to enable them to sell online. ITC will work with technical partner institutions specialized in digital commerce in Argentina, Chile, Colombia and Uruguay to develop and pilot with them tools and methodologies in Spanish. The project aims to guide and assess a total of 1,000 beneficiary companies in their e-commerce journey on the virtual learning platform developed by the project. Doing so, the initiative will contribute to SDG 5 and the ITC target of Connecting 3 Million of Women to Markets. SheTrades Latin America es una iniciativa piloto de SheTrades Global y ecomConnect del ITC que se está ejecutando entre junio 2021 y junio 2023. Su objetivo es aumentar la digitalización de las empresas latinoamericanas lideradas por mujeres para vender en línea. El ITC trabajará con las instituciones técnicas especializadas en comercio digital en Argentina, Chile, Colombia y Uruguay para desarrollar y probar las herramientas y metodologías en español. El proyecto aspira a capacitar a un total de 1.000 empresas beneficiarias en su ruta para vender en línea utilizando la plataforma de aprendizaje virtual desarrollada en el marco del proyecto. La iniciativa contribuirá al ODS 5 y al objetivo del ITC de Conectar a 3 millones de mujeres a los mercados.
ISIC name: Regulation of and contribution to more efficient operation of businesses

Project title: Support to the Implementation of the National Rural Development Policy
Description: The objective of this TC is to support the Ministry of Agriculture (MINAGRI by its Spanish acronym) and the Government of Chile in the implementation of the National Policy for Rural Development (NPRD) through: i) preparation and implementation of a public consultation process that will result in the definition of targets and specific activities to implement the NPDR, with a strong focus on gender and diversity; ii) preparation of a diagnostic of public investments with highest impact on rural areas; iii) preparation of an evaluation system of public investments in rural areas; and iv) identification of activities that will help the implementation of the NPDR.

Project title: Support to the Preparation of the Innovation and Institutional Strengthening for Food Security Program
Description: The objective of the technical cooperation is to support the preparation of a future Program for Innovation and Institutional Strengthening for Food Security (CH-L1171), which will seek to strengthen the functions of MINAGRI to protect and monitor essential assets (sanitary, genetic and natural resources) for food security; provide better quality services to farmers, agro-exporters and food importers; and support the digital transformation of the Servicio AgrÃ­cola y Ganadero (SAG) and the Instituto Nacional de Investigaciones Agropecuarias (INIA). The TC will support the design of the Program, since it will make it possible to define the priority investments and create the necessary capacities for the supervision and evaluation of the main results (KPIs) that the Program would have. In turn, the TC will contribute to strengthening the MINAGRI's skills budget preparation process, by generating products that will make it possible to substantiate the need for certain investments, which is essential for MINAGRI to have the budgetary space to include the IDB program.

Project title: Empresas Ariztia
Description: The proposed financing consists of a Long-term Senior Loan to Empresas Ariztía, for up to US$20 million and a maximum term of 10 years, for the company's investment program for the years 2021 and 2022, aimed at increasing production, implementing new technologies and efficiencies, improvements in terms of animal welfare, as well as their maintenance investments. Additionally, a second loan, which consists of a committed revolving line to finance working capital for up to US$30 million, with an availability period of 65 months, disbursements within 390 days, and intended to finance working capital related to the line bird genetics. Empresas Ariztía is one of the main poultry producers in Chile, with national and export sales, and value-added products in chickens, turkeys and cured meats. The company is vertically integrated in the production chain, from the production of the feed, through the incubation, fattening and processing of poultry meat, to the distribution and sale of the final product. In addition to poultry products, it also sells eggs, mushrooms, cheeses, butter, vegetables and other meats.

Project title: COPEVAL IMPORT FINANCE
Description: The proposed financing to Grupo Copeval consists of a 39-month revolving credit line, with a committed tranche and an uncommitted tranche for up to US$30 million, under which IDB Invest will extend financing for a term of up to 390 days with amortization at maturity, destined for the importation of machinery, inputs and raw materials, which were sold and financed to their agricultural producers. Copeval is the main distributor of agricultural inputs in Chile, with more than 65 years of experience in the market. It has a permanent stock of more than 28,700 products divided into the main lines of agrochemicals, fertilizers, food and grains, veterinary medicine, fuels and lubricants, machinery, seeds, technical irrigation, agricultural hardware, machinery rental and services. To carry out its operations, Copeval has a wide distribution network and a service model for all types of farmers, both multi-item and multi-size. The logistical efficiency of Copeval's distribution network allows it to deliver a comprehensive offer of agricultural inputs with a wide geographical coverage, serving more than 40,000 customers annually.

Project title: BRIMM Executive Micro Certificate in Economic Leadership 2023-2025: Online and In Person Educational Expansion/Micro-certificat exécutif en leadership économique du BRIMM 2023-2025 : Expansion de l'enseignement en ligne et en personne
Description: NRCan, through its Global Partnerships Initiative has provided $1-million over two years to the University of British Columbia’s (UBC) Bradshaw Research Institute for Minerals and Mining (BRIMM) to support the development and increased offering of its Executive Micro certificate in Economic Leadership for Mining program. The program will boost professional careers by teaching organizational leadership skills while addressing key challenges faced by mining and exploration companies worldwide. Funding will provide 300 scholarships targeting individuals who require financial support to access the program, making it more inclusive and accessible to a wider audience. BRIMM will also provide two in-person micro certificate programs for up to 50 students each in Argentina and Chile to grow economic development. The program will help address the current skilled labour shortages in the mining sector in Canada and many other mineral-rich countries. and strengthen professional networks. BRIMM micro certificate graduates will actively contribute to enhancing environmentally sustainable practices through conscious leadership in a world where business optimization and sustainability have become a priority for economic development. The program will help address the current skilled labour shortages in the mining sector in Canada and many other mineral-rich countries./RNCan, à travers de son Programme de partenariats internationaux (PPI), a accordé 1 million $ sur deux ans au Bradshaw Research Institute for Minerals and Mining (BRIMM) de l'Université de la Colombie-Britannique (UBC) pour soutenir le développement et l'augmentation de son programme de micro-certificat exécutif en leadership économique pour l'industrie minière. Ce programme stimulera les carrières professionnelles en enseignant des compétences de leadership organisationnel tout en abordant les principaux défis auxquels sont confrontées les sociétés d'exploitation et d'exploration minières dans le monde entier. Le financement permettra d'offrir 300 bourses à des personnes qui ont besoin d'un soutien financier pour accéder au programme, ce qui le rendra plus inclusif et accessible à un public plus large. Le BRIMM offrira également deux programmes de micro-certification en personne à 50 étudiants chacun, en Argentine et au Chili, afin de favoriser le développement économique. Le programme contribuera à combler les pénuries actuelles de main-d'œuvre qualifiée dans le secteur minier au Canada et dans de nombreux autres pays riches en minéraux. et à renforcer les réseaux professionnels. Les diplômés du micro-certificat du BRIMM contribueront activement à l'amélioration des pratiques durables sur le plan environnemental grâce à un leadership conscient dans un monde où l'optimisation et la durabilité des entreprises sont devenues une priorité pour le développement économique. Le programme contribuera à combler les pénuries actuelles de main-d'œuvre qualifiée dans le secteur minier au Canada et dans de nomb

Description: Promote the development and dissemination of culture and, in particular, the development of higher education and scientific and technological research between CETEM and the Universidad de Santiago de Chile. Project title: SheTrades Latin America: Supporting women businesses‘ transition towards the post COVID digital age (B999)
Description: SheTrades Latin America is a pilot initiative of ITC's SheTrades Global and ecomConnect programme running from June 2021 to June 2023 with the objective of increasing the digitalisation of women-led-businesses in Latin America to enable them to sell online. ITC will work with technical partner institutions specialized in digital commerce in Argentina, Chile, Colombia and Uruguay to develop and pilot with them tools and methodologies in Spanish. The project aims to guide and assess a total of 1,000 beneficiary companies in their e-commerce journey on the virtual learning platform developed by the project. Doing so, the initiative will contribute to SDG 5 and the ITC target of Connecting 3 Million of Women to Markets. SheTrades Latin America es una iniciativa piloto de SheTrades Global y ecomConnect del ITC que se está ejecutando entre junio 2021 y junio 2023. Su objetivo es aumentar la digitalización de las empresas latinoamericanas lideradas por mujeres para vender en línea. El ITC trabajará con las instituciones técnicas especializadas en comercio digital en Argentina, Chile, Colombia y Uruguay para desarrollar y probar las herramientas y metodologías en español. El proyecto aspira a capacitar a un total de 1.000 empresas beneficiarias en su ruta para vender en línea utilizando la plataforma de aprendizaje virtual desarrollada en el marco del proyecto. La iniciativa contribuirá al ODS 5 y al objetivo del ITC de Conectar a 3 millones de mujeres a los mercados.

Description: The proposed transaction consists on an A/B senior loan of up to US$150 million (the Loan) funded as follows: (i) an A loan of up of U$$30 million to be funded by IDB Invest; and (ii) a B loan of up to US$120 million to be funded by institutional investors The Loan will be amortizable in up to five years. Project title: Skills for Employment in the Extractives Sector of the Pacific Alliance/Compétences pour l'emploi dans le secteur extractif de l'Alliance du Pacifique
Description: The project supports Pacific Alliance countries (Chile, Colombia, Mexico and Peru) in strengthening their technical and vocational education and training, and establishing strong, demand-driven and industry-responsive training systems in the extractive sector.The project supports clean development and inclusive growth by collaborating with civil society and the private sector, and promoting responsible natural resource management and commercial practices. Project activities include: (1) providing training for the qualification and employability of vulnerable women, youth and Indigenous peoples, on environmental management and rehabilitation of mining sites; (2) sharing best practices in Canadian and Pacific Alliance institutions on self-employment; and (3) organizing thematic forums to disseminate policies developed on themes common to all Pacific Alliance countries. / Le projet appuie les pays de l’Alliance du Pacifique (Chili, Colombie, Mexique et Pérou) à renforcer l’enseignement et la formation techniques et professionnels et à mettre en place des systèmes de formation solides, axés sur la demande et adaptés aux besoins de l’industrie dans le secteur extractif.Le projet soutient le développement propre et la croissance inclusive en collaborant avec la société civile et le secteur privé et en encourageant une gestion responsable des ressources naturelles et des pratiques commerciales responsables. Parmi les activités du projet : 1) offrir une formation sur la qualification et l’employabilité des femmes, des jeunes et des peuples autochtones vulnérables sur la gestion de l’environnement et la réhabilitation des sites miniers; 2) partager les pratiques exemplaires des institutions du Canada et de l’Alliance du Pacifique sur le travail autonome; 3) organiser des forums thématiques pour diffuser les politiques élaborées sur des thèmes communs à tous les pays de l’Alliance du Pacifique.
ISIC name: Extraction of crude petroleum and natural gas

Project activities include: (1) providing training for the qualification and employability of vulnerable women, youth and Indigenous peoples, on environmental management and rehabilitation of mining sites; (2) sharing best practices in Canadian and Pacific Alliance institutions on self-employment; and (3) organizing thematic forums to disseminate policies developed on themes common to all Pacific Alliance countries. / Le projet appuie les pays de l’Alliance du Pacifique (Chili, Colombie, Mexique et Pérou) à renforcer l’enseignement et la formation techniques et professionnels et à mettre en place des systèmes de formation solides, axés sur la demande et adaptés aux besoins de l’industrie dans le secteur extractif.Le projet soutient le développement propre et la croissance inclusive en collaborant avec la société civile et le secteur privé et en encourageant une gestion responsable des ressources naturelles et des pratiques commerciales responsables. Parmi les activités du projet : 1) offrir une formation sur la qualification et l’employabilité des femmes, des jeunes et des peuples autochtones vulnérables sur la gestion de l’environnement et la réhabilitation des sites miniers; 2) partager les pratiques exemplaires des institutions du Canada et de l’Alliance du Pacifique sur le travail autonome; 3) organiser des forums thématiques pour diffuser les politiques élaborées sur des thèmes communs à tous les pays de l’Alliance du Pacifique.

Project title: Program to support Chiles Global Services Export Sector
Description: The overall objective of the program is to contribute to the development of the global export services sector in Chile. The specific objectives are: (i) increase the effectiveness and efficiency of the public sector to boost the global export services industry, reducing the current dispersion of efforts and increasing the link between the various institutions involved in export promotion and investment attraction, specially CORFO, ProChile and InvestChile; (ii) increase exports of global export services, especially by SMEs; (iii) increase foreign direct investment (FDI) in the global export services sector; and (iv) improve human resources capacities and increase the level of employment in the global export services sector.
